### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-03-31
Hi,
I just got an Asus MK241 display with DVI input. It's connected to an 8400GS and the monitor blinks randomly. It's very annoying when trying to work. Is this a driver issue...would another graphics card fix this? I would prefer not to connect via VGA. Here's xorg.conf...please help.
```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildd@crested)  Mon Nov  3 08:46:04 UTC 2008

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "ACI ASUS MK241"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 80.0
    VertRefresh     55.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 8400 GS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1920x1200_60 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```

### Post by deanfox on 2009-04-09
I'm not sure but I have what sounds like the same problem.  I have a brand new system with a 8400GS and upon installing 8.10 AMD 64 Desktop I'm getting a blinking screen. Every few seconds it will blink out, there's a DVI Asus thing in the upper left corner it may or may not go away and then it blinks off again.  It's going to be very difficult to even fix this problem with the screen blanking every few seconds.

I'm in the initial stages of research and your's is the first post I've seen so far.  I'll try and finish the install. Maybe try the install again with VGA, etc.  I'll let you know if I stumble onto anything.  You posted a week ago. If your's is as bad as mine you've either found a fix or have gone back to another card, VGA, etc.  It's unusable as is.

-[d]-

### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-04-10
I've gotten it to stop doing this by turning off all eyecandy. Essentially, going to Preferences -> Appearance -> Visual Effects and selecting None. That seems to work. 

I haven't tried rolling back the driver (kernel module). In fact, I'm not really sure which one I am using. I haven't tried VGA (My Asus display supports it).

On a related note, I had the same issue with XP, that is until I updated the driver with the new one from Nvidia. I installed XP on an extra drive and went through all the ballyhoo of getting drivers for everything (on board sound, on board gigabit, video card, etc). It blinked until I updated the video card drivers.

Maybe this will be fixed with 9.04? Ideas?

--stitch

---

### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-04-11
So I have a little more time to play with this. Eventually the blinking returned. I rolled back the driver from 177 to 173 and the blinking is still happening. In fact, every time I reboot my machine I have to reset the resolution to 1920x1200 because it automatically sets itself to 1600x1200.

I am going to stop using the drivers all together and see if that works. It must be a driver bug.

--stitch

---

### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-04-11
After a little work and some reconfiguring of X manually, I think I have it stable. I am not using the nvidia driver so no compiz, sorry. But here's the X config that works for me.

/etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nv"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "ASUS"
    ModelName      "ACI ASUS MK241"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "1920x1200 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection
```
-stitch

---

### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-04-26
I tested this with a new video card, that I borrowed from a friend, (9800 GT) and it worked fine all weekend. It must have been a hardware issue. I just purchased a 9800 GTX and it works flawlessly.

### Post by quantumstitch on 2009-12-24
Are you using the DVI port on the card to connect to your display? 

I purchased a different card (9800GTX) and it works great (see above). But, I have tested using the VGA port on the 8400GS. That seems to work just fine with and without Compiz.

HTH,
